semantic.role: Describes the automated build-watcher script for the ArchSpine project's development workflow
responsibilities: Explains how the script watches key source files for changes
Describes the polling mechanism and signature computation
Covers build triggering, queuing, and debouncing behavior
Documents the rebuild lifecycle (initial, file change, queued)
outOfScope: Does not cover build configuration, transpiler options, or deployment
Does not describe the ArchSpine mirror system itself
Does not include testing or linting watchers

English.purpose: This document details the `dev:build` watcher script that automatically rebuilds the ArchSpine project when source files change. It explains the polling strategy, signature-based change detection, and the queued build mechanism that prevents overlapping rebuilds.
English.context_and_audience: Intended for developers maintaining or contributing to the ArchSpine project. This script is part of the local development environment, ensuring that builds stay current without manual intervention.
key_takeaways: The watcher polls a defined set of files every 1000ms, comparing file modification timestamps and sizes.
A single build queue ensures no concurrent rebuilds; changes during a build are queued for the next cycle.
The script uses a signature (concatenation of file paths, mtime, and size) to detect changes.
Initial build runs immediately on start, then watches for subsequent changes.
